query: What is the capacity threshold that triggers the large generation interconnection process in NYISO according to the NYISO process manual 23 and guide UG21? Cite the relevant source report and section you got this from
final_answer:
## Answer: **20 MW**

The capacity threshold that triggers the large generation interconnection process in NYISO is **20 MW**. Generating facilities with a total capacity **exceeding 20 MW** are subject to the Large Facility Interconnection Procedures (LFIP), while facilities of **20 MW or less** are processed under the Small Generator Interconnection Procedures (SGIP).

### Relevant Sources and Citations

1. **NYISO OATT Attachment X (Standard Large Facility Interconnection Procedures)** — The tariff itself is titled: *"30 Attachment X – Standard Large Facility Interconnection Procedures (Applicable to Generating Facilities that exceed 20 MWs and to Class Year Transmission Facilities)."* 
   - **Source:** [NYISO OATT Section 30 – Attachment X](https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/33257202/OATT%20Section%2030%20-%20Full.pdf)

2. **NYISO OATT Attachment Z (Small Generator Interconnection Procedures)** — The SGIP tariff is titled: *"32 Attachment Z – Small Generator Interconnection Procedures (SGIP) (Applicable to Generating Facilities No Larger Than 20 MW)."*
   - **Source:** [NYISO OATT Section 32 – Attachment Z](https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/33257202/OATT%2032%20MT_Order%202222%20Compliance%20Revisions.pdf)

3. **NYISO Manual 23 (Transmission Expansion and Interconnection Manual), Section 3.4.1.1 ("What projects are subject to the SGIP?")** — As summarized by the DOE's OpenEI RAPID Toolkit, which cites Manual 23 directly: *"The SGIP applies to a proposal to increase the capability of an existing generation facility only if the resultant size of the facility is 20 MW or less. A proposal to increase the capability of an existing generation facility would fall under the Large Generator Interconnection Procedures (LGIP) if the resultant size of the facility is more than 20 MW, even though the incremental increase in capability may be less than 20 MW."*
   - **Source:** [OpenEI RAPID/Roadmap/8-NY-h](https://openei.org/wiki/RAPID/Roadmap/8-NY-h), citing **NYISO Transmission Expansion and Interconnection Manual, at § 3.4.1.1**

4. **NYISO Manual 23, Section 3.3.1.1 ("What projects are subject to the LFIP?")** — The LFIP applies to generating facilities that exceed 20 MW and to Class Year Transmission Facilities. (Referenced in the Manual 23 Table of Contents.)
   - **Source:** [NYISO Manual 23 (v4.0, August 2019)](https://documents.dps.ny.gov/public/Common/ViewDoc.aspx?DocRefId=%7B80BDC927-CF2E-43E8-8A04-847D26B4E8E7%7D), Sections 3.3.1 and 3.4.1

5. **NYISO UG-21 (Transmission Expansion and Interconnection User's Guide), Section 6** — References the Standard Interconnection Procedures (SIP) and notes requirements for *"Generating Facilities greater than 20 MW,"* including attestations required by NYSRC Reliability Rule B.5.
   - **Source:** [NYISO UG-21 Guide v3.0 (July 2025)](https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/3625950/UG-21-TEI+Guide-v2-0-Final.pdf), Section 6

### Summary of the Threshold

| Facility Capacity | Applicable NYISO Process | Governing Tariff |
|---|---|---|
| **> 20 MW** | Large Facility Interconnection Procedures (LFIP) | OATT Attachment X |
| **≤ 20 MW** | Small Generator Interconnection Procedures (SGIP) | OATT Attachment Z |

> **Note:** In May 2024, NYISO filed revisions under FERC Order No. 2023 (Docket ER24-1915) establishing new Standard Interconnection Procedures in OATT Attachment HH, which replace the legacy LFIP and SGIP frameworks. As of the February 2025 version of Manual 23, the Section 3 LFIP/SGIP procedures only continue to apply to projects completing Class Year 2023 studies and Small Generator facilities studies under the transition rules. However, the **20 MW threshold** remains the fundamental dividing line between "large" and "small" generator interconnection treatment.

steps.reasoning:
Now I have gathered enough information to provide a comprehensive answer. Let me synthesize the findings.

The key question is about the capacity threshold that triggers the large generation interconnection process in NYISO according to Manual 23 and Guide UG-21.

From the search results, I can see that:

1. From the NYISO OATT Attachment X (Standard Large Facility Interconnection Procedures): "Applicable to Generating Facilities that exceed 20 MWs and to Class Year Transmission Facilities" - this is the tariff language.

2. From the OpenEI source, which cites the NYISO Transmission Expansion and Interconnection Manual at §3.4.1.1: "The SGIP applies to a proposal to increase the capability of an existing generation facility only if the resultant size of the facility is 20 MW or less. A proposal to increase the capability of an existing generation facility would fall under the Large Generator Interconnection Procedures (LGIP) if the resultant size of the facility is more than 20 MW, even though the incremental increase in capability may be less than 20 MW."

3. From the NYISO OATT Attachment Z (SGIP): "Applicable To Generating Facilities No Larger Than 20 MW"

4. The UG-21 guide references "Attestations (for Generating Facilities greater than 20 MW)" in the Standard Interconnection Procedures context.

So the threshold is **20 MW**. Generating facilities that exceed 20 MW are subject to the Large Facility Interconnection Procedures (LFIP) / Standard Large Facility Interconnection Procedures, while facilities of 20 MW or less are subject to the Small Generator Interconnection Procedures (SGIP).

The Manual 23 (version 4.0, August 2019) has:
- Section 3.3: Large Facility Interconnection Procedures (LFIP)
- Section 3.3.1.1: "What projects are subject to the LFIP?"
- Section 3.4: Small Generator Interconnection Procedures (SGIP)
- Section 3.4.1.1: "What projects are subject to the SGIP?"

And the UG-21 Guide, Section 6 discusses the Standard Interconnection Procedures (SIP) and references "Generating Facilities greater than 20 MW" requiring attestations.
